query: What are the basic requirements to be admitted to a master's programme at the University of Vienna?

queries: What are the basic requirements to be admitted to a master's programme at the University of Vienna?

retrieved.title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?
heading_path: I want to study a master programme
General requirements for the application for admission to master programmes
retrieved.text:
- scan of your valid passport or personal ID (front and back) as JPEG or PNG file
- - higher education degree certificate and transcripts/diploma supplement
    - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
    - and a sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)

  - for German and multilingual programmes: [proof of German language proficiency](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) at level A2 or higher
- if applicable proof of [English language proficiency/knowledge of other languages](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/) according to the curriculum
- further documents specified in the [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/#c124164) (e.g. description of qualifications)

You can find information regarding further requirements (e.g. qualitative admission requirements, letter of motivation, language proficiency, ...) on the overview page of the individual degree programme. To find the overview choose the programme you are interested in from the list at [Admission > Master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/).

You have to submit your application for admission to the degree programme within the application period. The application period depends on your citizenships. [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069) have a special application period.

retrieved.title: Master Access Guide (MAG)
retrieved.text:
Do you want to study a master programme at the University of Vienna? The prerequisite for this is the completion of an eligible bachelor or diploma programme (= in the same discipline) at a post-secondary educational institution.

In the MAG you’ll learn:

- which degree programmes are considered as eligible for your chosen degree programme
- if you need to fulfil any qualitative admission prerequisites for admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.
- if you need to fulfil any supplementary  examinations after admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.

If your previous degree is not listed in the Master Access Guide of the respective degree programme, your application will be checked individually as a decision has to be made on a case-by-case basis. To do this, you must apply for admission to the degree programme by the relevant deadline. The University of Vienna can only assess the eligibility of your previous degree on the basis of your complete application. Preliminary assessments are not possible.

The admission requirements according to university law and the applicable master [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) are binding.

[## You've got further questions?](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

[You get further information at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme.](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

retrieved.title: Information for asylum seekers
heading_path: How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
retrieved.text: How do I get admitted to a degree programme? In order to be admitted to a bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna you are required to possess a secondary-school leaving certificate or to have completed a degree programme of at least three years. Admission to master programmes require the completion of a subject-related bachelor programme. - You need to provide the required documents in their original or legalised form. If you no longer possess your secondary-school leaving certificate or your higher education degree certificate, you may request a substitute confirmation at the [Federal Ministry Women, Science and Research Republic of Austria](https://www.bmfwf.gv.at/public.html). Admission is not possible without a secondary-school leaving certificate or higher education degree certificate. - Documents issued in a language other than German or English additionally require a legally attested translation. Your documents do not require [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/), irrespective of the issuing country. However, you need to prove your status as a recognised refugee/asylum seeker (e.g. by means of a convention travel document). You can submit your application for admission online on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/). This is only possible during the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The University of Vienna conducts an [entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) for select bachelor/diploma programmes. An entrance exam procedure is also conducted for admission to the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. You can apply for admission to these programmes only once per academic year, in spring. The entrance

retrieved.title: Admission to master programmes
retrieved.text:
Choose a degree programme to receive detailed information regarding the admission procedure. Your steps vary depending on

- if you have completed your bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna or another university, and
- if there are any qualitative admission requirements (QZB) prescribed in the curriculum, and
- if a selection procedure (AV for "Aufnahmeverfahren") is conducted for the master programme of your choice, and
- if the language of instruction of the degree programme is English or German.

Use the selection below. There you will find a step-by-step introduction on how to submit your application and complete the admission procedure.

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: D
retrieved.text: programmes, you will need subject-specific prior knowledge. You must provide proof of this when you apply for admission. Proof is provided either in the form of a trancript of records from the University of Vienna or a description of your qualifications (= a description of your achievements). The curricula of the master programmes or the Master Access Guide (MAG) indicate which extension curricula (EC) will provide you with the subject-specific prior knowledge. You have the following options: 1. If you have been admitted to a Bachelor's programme at the University of Vienna, you will already complete the required EC during the course of this programme. You do not need a qualification description, your transcript of records is sufficient proof. 2. You did not study at the University of Vienna? Compare the curriculum of your degree programme with the admission requirements listed in the EC curriculum and enter the relevant achievements from your previous degree programme in the "Description of Qualifications" form. Upload the form as proof with your application for admission. Related links: - [Master Access Guide (MAG)](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/#c167271) - [Admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876049) - [List of all curricula](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) (German only) - Diploma programme In 1999, the Bologna Process was initiated by the European Higher Education Area (EHEA). This fundamentally changed the university structure in Austria. Diploma programmes have been replaced by bachelor and master programmes. At the University of Vienna,

retrieved.title: Additional master programme
heading_path: Master programmes without entrance exam procedure
Note for graduates of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

retrieved.title: Information for asylum seekers
retrieved.text:
Asylum seekers, convention refugees, and people granted subsidiary protection who possess a secondary-school leaving certificate and/or a university degree from a non-EU/EEA country may apply for admission to a degree programme at the University of Vienna. The requirements are as follows: you must fulfil the admission requirements and adhere to the [application and admission period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/).

The University of Vienna offers over 150 bachelor, master and doctoral programmes. You can get more detailed information about the programmes offered on the following pages:

- [All degree programmes](/en/degree-programmes/)
- [StudiesServiceCenters (SSC) and StudiesServiceUnits (SSS) of the degree programmes](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)
- [Students’ Union (ÖH) of the University of Vienna](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [Center for Doctoral Studies of the University of Vienna](https://doktorat.univie.ac.at/en/)

retrieved.title: Master Programmes

Here you find a list of all master programmes offered at the University of Vienna.

The instruction language of most master degree programmes is [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/). Information about [programmes in another language can be found here](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/).

Information about the application process can be found here.

For some degree programmes you need to pass an entrance examination

- [Teacher Education Programme (bachelor and master)](/en/degree-programmes/teacher-education-programme/)

retrieved.title: Competence check at VFS Global
heading_path: Step 1: Inform yourself about the admission requirements
retrieved.text:
Find out in advance which requirements you must meet with documents from your country in order to fulfil the admission requirements at the University of Vienna. The competence check only makes sense if you meet all the requirements.

Prospective students who do not fulfil all requirements cannot be admitted to a degree programme at the University of Vienna.

Also check the specific requirements for your desired degree programme, such as proof of German and/or English language proficiency and further required documents. You can find information on this on the [page for the respective degree programme](/en/admission-procedure/).

Please also ensure that your documents are correctly legalised and translated.

- [Documents from Bangladesh](#c1270047)
- [Documents from India](#c1270039)
- [Documents from Pakistan](#c1270031)

Your documents have to be legalised by [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121827) and must be translated into either German or English afterwards.

Your steps in the admission procedure depend on the degree programme of your choice.

retrieved.title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?
heading_path: I want to study a master programme
Country-specific info
retrieved.text: / - African countries The Higher National Diploma does not fulfil the requirements for admission to a master programme. --- Special case Nigeria: The Austrian embassy or the Austrian consulate require a proof of the University of Vienna, before the over-legalisation can be issued? Proceed as follows: 1. Let your documents be legalised by the ministry of education. 2. Let the documents that were legalised by the ministry of education be legalised by the ministry of foreign affairs. 3. Apply for admission to the degree programme with these documents within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). You will receive an automatic confirmation, that you have applied for admission at the University of Vienna via e-mail. 4. Provide this confirmation at the Austrian representation and let your documents be over-legalised. This automatic confirmation is accepted as proof. 5. A few weeks after your application you will receive an amendment request with a deadline for submission (as the over-legalisaiton by the Austrian representation was missing in your original submission). 6. Submit the documents with the full diplomatic certification within the deadline. 7. Wait for your admission letter. - Afghanistan If you have only completed a bachelor programme in Afghanistan, you cannot be admitted to a master programme at the University of Vienna. The differences regarding the level of education are too large. Your application for admission will therefore be rejected. --- Exception: If your bachelor

retrieved.title: Non-degree programme to make up for qualitative admission requirements
heading_path: Admission to a non-degree programme
retrieved.text:
1. Register in u:space
   If this is your first time studying at the University of Vienna: Activate your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/) within the application period. Make sure you remember the login data, because you will further need the u:account during your studies. Log in to u:space, complete the registration.
2. Apply for admission to a non-degree programme
   Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/61/create/1644) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
3. Wait for an answer
   Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
4. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee.
   The admission is completed as soon as the [prescribed fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can validate your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) or order it if you do not yet have one.

Register for the required courses. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

retrieved.title: Attending courses out of interest
retrieved.text:
You are interested in individual courses at the University of Vienna and want to further your education without completing a degree programme? Then a non-degree programme is the right choice.

The information on this website applies to persons who are admitted at the University of Vienna for the first time. If you were admitted to a degree or non-degree programme at the University of Vienna in the past, please read the information on the [resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/).

You need the following document as JPEG or PNG file:

- your valid passport or personal ID card (front and back)

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application and admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application and admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

retrieved.title: Additional degree programmes
retrieved.text:
You are currently admitted to a degree programme at the University of Vienna and would like to study an additional degree programme? Your steps in the admission procedure and the application period depend on the type of degree programme.

[## Bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/admission/additional-degree-programmes/additional-bachelordiploma-programme/)

[## Master programmes](/en/admission/additional-degree-programmes/additional-master-programme/)

- Number of degree programmes: According to the regulations of the University of Vienna, you can be admitted to an unlimited number of degree programmes. Please note that studying multiple degree programmes successfully and actively entails a higher effort regarding [study organisation](/en/study-organisation/). All degree programmes offered at the University of Vienna are full-time programmes. There is no distinction between main and secondary degree programme. Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions regarding family allowance and/or study grants.
- Recognition of exams: The [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) of the respective degree programme can provide information regarding recognition of prior study achievements and help you to organise your studies.
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.

- [Information regarding admission to a third teaching subject as complementary degree programme "Erweiterungsstudium drittes Unterrichtsfach" (only available in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/lehramtsstudien/erweiterungsstudium/#c277199)

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: S
retrieved.text: The University of Vienna conducts selection procedures for some master programmes. This mainly applies to English-language programmes. For some master programmes, you must provide proof of qualitative admission requirements ("qualitative Zulassungsbedingungen") and/or upload a letter of motivation when applying for admission. These programmes have their own application deadlines (always in spring, for the following academic year). Related links: - [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069) Related entries: - [Application period](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30198&cHash=83dcf3f2a3a1fe41d6eef2f91f47ba7c) - Semester abroad As a degree student you can pass one or more semesters abroad at a different university (Outgoing Mobility). The University of Vienna cooperates with numerous partner universities abroad. There are also several mobility programmes offered for incoming students. Related Links: - [International Office](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/) Related entries: - [CEEPUS](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29987&cHash=72c1e3c31b64c2842340511847949735) - [Free Mover](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29995&cHash=5488b37118f3efe558bb850a9466ab5d) - [Recognition of exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30187&cHash=7ad03787bb35116164cf00dbe2cdd76a) - [International Office](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30849&cHash=6aab915f8821fb9ddb9f2a36c1d8438f) - [Erasmus+](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30864&cHash=ea57c8821cd2999c69dcc17641651743) - [Mobility programmes](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31188&cHash=813707a12daceba2774512659b917d60) - [Visiting Master](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31337&cHash=70e303dffac1661c3aafc82b37c76b70) - Seminar (course type) Related entries: - [Course with continuous assessment (pi LV)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31243&cHash=04d9e48aefae1365779d07a302248e56) - Senate The Senate consists of 18 members. Together with the Rectorate and the University Board, it forms the governing body of the University of Vienna. Every three years, the Senate is elected by the academic and non-academic university staff. Related Links: - [Senate of the University of Vienna (in German)](https://senat.univie.ac.at/infos-zum-senat/) Related entries: - [Statutes](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31252&cHash=9dc01c87dda397fab5d23352329e6a42) - Special libraries The special libraries belong to the University Library, however, they are located in the different institutes. There you can find literature related

retrieved.title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?
heading_path: FAQ
retrieved.text: / - Can I apply without the required legalisation (according to the issuing country)? No, this is not possible. You have to fulfil the formal requirements. This means that you must provide all required documents in your application for admission to the degree programme. Only apply for admission, if you fulfil all requirements and if you have all required documents. - I don't have my bachelor certificate yet. Can I already apply for admission to a master programme? You can only apply for admission after you have entirely completed your degree programme and have lost the entitlement to take exams. However, you do not have to have the final documents confirming the completion of your studies. The University of Vienna also accepts a confirmation issued by your educational institution about the completion of studies in combination with your final transcripts/diploma supplement, as long as this also contains the legalisation required for your country. Confirmations that you will complete your degree programme cannot be accepted (only exception: master programmes with selection exam procedure). Provisional transcripts cannot be accepted as well. - Can I already know which supplementary exams I will have to pass, before I submit my application for admission? Unfortunately, this is not possible as which and if any supplementary examinations are prescribed will only be determined individually during the processing of your documents. - I already have a university

retrieved.title: Studying a non-degree programme at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
- Nostrification: Detailed info on the requirements for and procedure of the nostrification is available on the website of the [Büro Studienpräses](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/en/nostrification/). You will be admitted to a non-degree programme for the purpose of establishing equivalency.
- Continuing education: On the website of the [Postgraduate Center](https://postgraduatecenter.univie.ac.at/en/) you will find information on the requirements for admission to continuing education courses, certification courses and a lot more.
- Exchange and mobility programmes: Detailed info on mobility programmes of the University of Vienna is available on the website of the [International Office](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/).

reranked.title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?
heading_path: I want to study a master programme
General requirements for the application for admission to master programmes
reranked.text:
- scan of your valid passport or personal ID (front and back) as JPEG or PNG file
- - higher education degree certificate and transcripts/diploma supplement
    - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
    - and a sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)

  - for German and multilingual programmes: [proof of German language proficiency](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) at level A2 or higher

You can find information regarding further requirements (e.g. qualitative admission requirements, letter of motivation, language proficiency, ...) on the overview page of the individual degree programme. You have to submit your application for admission to the degree programme within the application period. The application period depends on your citizenships. [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069) have a special application period.

reranked.title: Master Access Guide (MAG)
reranked.text:
Do you want to study a master programme at the University of Vienna? The prerequisite for this is the completion of an eligible bachelor or diploma programme (= in the same discipline) at a post-secondary educational institution.

In the MAG you’ll learn:

- which degree programmes are considered as eligible for your chosen degree programme
- if you need to fulfil any qualitative admission prerequisites for admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.
- if you need to fulfil any supplementary  examinations after admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.

If your previous degree is not listed in the Master Access Guide of the respective degree programme, your application will be checked individually as a decision has to be made on a case-by-case basis. To do this, you must apply for admission to the degree programme by the relevant deadline. The University of Vienna can only assess the eligibility of your previous degree on the basis of your complete application. Preliminary assessments are not possible.

The admission requirements according to university law and the applicable master [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) are binding.

[## You've got further questions?](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

[You get further information at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme.](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

heading_path: How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
reranked.text: How do I get admitted to a degree programme? In order to be admitted to a bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna you are required to possess a secondary-school leaving certificate or to have completed a degree programme of at least three years. Admission to master programmes require the completion of a subject-related bachelor programme. - You need to provide the required documents in their original or legalised form. If you no longer possess your secondary-school leaving certificate or your higher education degree certificate, you may request a substitute confirmation at the [Federal Ministry Women, Science and Research Republic of Austria](https://www.bmfwf.gv.at/public.html). Admission is not possible without a secondary-school leaving certificate or higher education degree certificate. - Documents issued in a language other than German or English additionally require a legally attested translation. Your documents do not require [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/), irrespective of the issuing country. However, you need to prove your status as a recognised refugee/asylum seeker (e.g. by means of a convention travel document). You can submit your application for admission online on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/). This is only possible during the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The University of Vienna conducts an [entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) for select bachelor/diploma programmes. An entrance exam procedure is also conducted for admission to the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. You can apply for admission to these programmes only once per academic year, in spring. The entrance

reranked.title: Admission to master programmes
reranked.text:
Choose a degree programme to receive detailed information regarding the admission procedure. Your steps vary depending on

- if you have completed your bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna or another university, and
- if there are any qualitative admission requirements (QZB) prescribed in the curriculum, and
- if a selection procedure (AV for "Aufnahmeverfahren") is conducted for the master programme of your choice, and
- if the language of instruction of the degree programme is English or German.

Use the selection below. There you will find a step-by-step introduction on how to submit your application and complete the admission procedure.

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: D
reranked.text: programmes, you will need subject-specific prior knowledge. You must provide proof of this when you apply for admission. Proof is provided either in the form of a trancript of records from the University of Vienna or a description of your qualifications (= a description of your achievements). The curricula of the master programmes or the Master Access Guide (MAG) indicate which extension curricula (EC) will provide you with the subject-specific prior knowledge. You have the following options: 1. If you have been admitted to a Bachelor's programme at the University of Vienna, you will already complete the required EC during the course of this programme. You do not need a qualification description, your transcript of records is sufficient proof. 2. You did not study at the University of Vienna? Compare the curriculum of your degree programme with the admission requirements listed in the EC curriculum and enter the relevant achievements from your previous degree programme in the "Description of Qualifications" form. Upload the form as proof with your application for admission. Related links: - [Master Access Guide (MAG)](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/#c167271) - [Admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876049) - [List of all curricula](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) (German only) - Diploma programme In 1999, the Bologna Process was initiated by the European Higher Education Area (EHEA). This fundamentally changed the university structure in Austria. Diploma programmes have been replaced by bachelor and master programmes. At the University of Vienna,

reranked.title: Additional master programme
heading_path: Master programmes without entrance exam procedure
Note for graduates of the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

reranked.title: Information for asylum seekers
reranked.text:
Asylum seekers, convention refugees, and people granted subsidiary protection who possess a secondary-school leaving certificate and/or a university degree from a non-EU/EEA country may apply for admission to a degree programme at the University of Vienna. The requirements are as follows: you must fulfil the admission requirements and adhere to the [application and admission period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/).

The University of Vienna offers over 150 bachelor, master and doctoral programmes. You can get more detailed information about the programmes offered on the following pages:

- [All degree programmes](/en/degree-programmes/)
- [StudiesServiceCenters (SSC) and StudiesServiceUnits (SSS) of the degree programmes](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)
- [Students’ Union (ÖH) of the University of Vienna](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [Center for Doctoral Studies of the University of Vienna](https://doktorat.univie.ac.at/en/)

Which degree programme are you interested in?

This applies regardless of your results in the competence check. [Detailed information about admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/)

reranked.title: Master Programmes
reranked.text:
Here you find a list of all master programmes offered at the University of Vienna.

The instruction language of most master degree programmes is [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/). Information about [programmes in another language can be found here](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/).

Information about the application process can be found here.

For some degree programmes you need to pass an entrance examination

- [Teacher Education Programme (bachelor and master)](/en/degree-programmes/teacher-education-programme/)

reranked.title: Competence check at VFS Global
heading_path: Step 1: Inform yourself about the admission requirements
reranked.text:
Find out in advance which requirements you must meet with documents from your country in order to fulfil the admission requirements at the University of Vienna. The competence check only makes sense if you meet all the requirements.

Prospective students who do not fulfil all requirements cannot be admitted to a degree programme at the University of Vienna.

Also check the specific requirements for your desired degree programme, such as proof of German and/or English language proficiency and further required documents. You can find information on this on the [page for the respective degree programme](/en/admission-procedure/).

Please also ensure that your documents are correctly legalised and translated.

- [Documents from Bangladesh](#c1270047)
- [Documents from India](#c1270039)
- [Documents from Pakistan](#c1270031)

Your documents have to be legalised by [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121827) and must be translated into either German or English afterwards.

Your steps in the admission procedure depend on the degree programme of your choice.

reranked.title: Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme
heading_path: Different master programme without selection procedure
Note for graduates of the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

reranked.title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?
heading_path: I want to study a master programme
Country-specific info
reranked.text: / - African countries The Higher National Diploma does not fulfil the requirements for admission to a master programme. --- Special case Nigeria: The Austrian embassy or the Austrian consulate require a proof of the University of Vienna, before the over-legalisation can be issued? Proceed as follows: 1. Let your documents be legalised by the ministry of education. 2. Let the documents that were legalised by the ministry of education be legalised by the ministry of foreign affairs. 3. Apply for admission to the degree programme with these documents within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). You will receive an automatic confirmation, that you have applied for admission at the University of Vienna via e-mail. 4. Provide this confirmation at the Austrian representation and let your documents be over-legalised. This automatic confirmation is accepted as proof. 5. A few weeks after your application you will receive an amendment request with a deadline for submission (as the over-legalisaiton by the Austrian representation was missing in your original submission). 6. Submit the documents with the full diplomatic certification within the deadline. 7. Wait for your admission letter. - Afghanistan If you have only completed a bachelor programme in Afghanistan, you cannot be admitted to a master programme at the University of Vienna. The differences regarding the level of education are too large. Your application for admission will therefore be rejected. --- Exception: If your bachelor

reranked.title: Non-degree programme to make up for qualitative admission requirements
heading_path: Admission to a non-degree programme
reranked.text:
1. Register in u:space
   If this is your first time studying at the University of Vienna: Activate your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/) within the application period. Make sure you remember the login data, because you will further need the u:account during your studies. Log in to u:space, complete the registration.
2. Apply for admission to a non-degree programme
   Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/61/create/1644) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
3. Wait for an answer
   Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
4. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee.
   The admission is completed as soon as the [prescribed fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can validate your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) or order it if you do not yet have one.

Register for the required courses. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

reranked.title: Attending courses out of interest
reranked.text:
You are interested in individual courses at the University of Vienna and want to further your education without completing a degree programme? Then a non-degree programme is the right choice.

The information on this website applies to persons who are admitted at the University of Vienna for the first time. If you were admitted to a degree or non-degree programme at the University of Vienna in the past, please read the information on the [resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/).

You need the following document as JPEG or PNG file:

- your valid passport or personal ID card (front and back)

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application and admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application and admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

reranked.title: Additional degree programmes
reranked.text:
You are currently admitted to a degree programme at the University of Vienna and would like to study an additional degree programme? Your steps in the admission procedure and the application period depend on the type of degree programme.

[## Bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/admission/additional-degree-programmes/additional-bachelordiploma-programme/)

[## Master programmes](/en/admission/additional-degree-programmes/additional-master-programme/)

- Number of degree programmes: According to the regulations of the University of Vienna, you can be admitted to an unlimited number of degree programmes. Please note that studying multiple degree programmes successfully and actively entails a higher effort regarding [study organisation](/en/study-organisation/). All degree programmes offered at the University of Vienna are full-time programmes. There is no distinction between main and secondary degree programme. Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions regarding family allowance and/or study grants.
- Recognition of exams: The [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) of the respective degree programme can provide information regarding recognition of prior study achievements and help you to organise your studies.
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.

- [Information regarding admission to a third teaching subject as complementary degree programme "Erweiterungsstudium drittes Unterrichtsfach" (only available in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/lehramtsstudien/erweiterungsstudium/#c277199)

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: S
reranked.text: The University of Vienna conducts selection procedures for some master programmes. This mainly applies to English-language programmes. For some master programmes, you must provide proof of qualitative admission requirements ("qualitative Zulassungsbedingungen") and/or upload a letter of motivation when applying for admission. These programmes have their own application deadlines (always in spring, for the following academic year). Related links: - [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069) Related entries: - [Application period](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30198&cHash=83dcf3f2a3a1fe41d6eef2f91f47ba7c) - Semester abroad As a degree student you can pass one or more semesters abroad at a different university (Outgoing Mobility). The University of Vienna cooperates with numerous partner universities abroad. There are also several mobility programmes offered for incoming students. Related Links: - [International Office](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/) Related entries: - [CEEPUS](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29987&cHash=72c1e3c31b64c2842340511847949735) - [Free Mover](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29995&cHash=5488b37118f3efe558bb850a9466ab5d) - [Recognition of exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30187&cHash=7ad03787bb35116164cf00dbe2cdd76a) - [International Office](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30849&cHash=6aab915f8821fb9ddb9f2a36c1d8438f) - [Erasmus+](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30864&cHash=ea57c8821cd2999c69dcc17641651743) - [Mobility programmes](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31188&cHash=813707a12daceba2774512659b917d60) - [Visiting Master](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31337&cHash=70e303dffac1661c3aafc82b37c76b70) - Seminar (course type) Related entries: - [Course with continuous assessment (pi LV)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31243&cHash=04d9e48aefae1365779d07a302248e56) - Senate The Senate consists of 18 members. Together with the Rectorate and the University Board, it forms the governing body of the University of Vienna. Every three years, the Senate is elected by the academic and non-academic university staff. Related Links: - [Senate of the University of Vienna (in German)](https://senat.univie.ac.at/infos-zum-senat/) Related entries: - [Statutes](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31252&cHash=9dc01c87dda397fab5d23352329e6a42) - Special libraries The special libraries belong to the University Library, however, they are located in the different institutes. There you can find literature related

reranked.title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?
heading_path: FAQ
reranked.text: / - Can I apply without the required legalisation (according to the issuing country)? No, this is not possible. You have to fulfil the formal requirements. This means that you must provide all required documents in your application for admission to the degree programme. Only apply for admission, if you fulfil all requirements and if you have all required documents. - I don't have my bachelor certificate yet. Can I already apply for admission to a master programme? You can only apply for admission after you have entirely completed your degree programme and have lost the entitlement to take exams. However, you do not have to have the final documents confirming the completion of your studies. The University of Vienna also accepts a confirmation issued by your educational institution about the completion of studies in combination with your final transcripts/diploma supplement, as long as this also contains the legalisation required for your country. Confirmations that you will complete your degree programme cannot be accepted (only exception: master programmes with selection exam procedure). Provisional transcripts cannot be accepted as well. - Can I already know which supplementary exams I will have to pass, before I submit my application for admission? Unfortunately, this is not possible as which and if any supplementary examinations are prescribed will only be determined individually during the processing of your documents. - I already have a university

reranked.title: Studying a non-degree programme at the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
- Nostrification: Detailed info on the requirements for and procedure of the nostrification is available on the website of the [Büro Studienpräses](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/en/nostrification/). You will be admitted to a non-degree programme for the purpose of establishing equivalency.
- Continuing education: On the website of the [Postgraduate Center](https://postgraduatecenter.univie.ac.at/en/) you will find information on the requirements for admission to continuing education courses, certification courses and a lot more.
- Exchange and mobility programmes: Detailed info on mobility programmes of the University of Vienna is available on the website of the [International Office](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/).

- [Amount and payment of tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/)
- [Tips on how to create your semester schedule](/en/semester-planning/)
- [Detailed information about the u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en)
- [Studying and Living – Who can help me with...?](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/)

selected.title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?
heading_path: I want to study a master programme
General requirements for the application for admission to master programmes
selected.text:
- scan of your valid passport or personal ID (front and back) as JPEG or PNG file
- - higher education degree certificate and transcripts/diploma supplement
    - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
    - and a sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)

  - for German and multilingual programmes: [proof of German language proficiency](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) at level A2 or higher
- if applicable proof of [English language proficiency/knowledge of other languages](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/) according to the curriculum
- further documents specified in the [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/#c124164) (e.g. description of qualifications)

You can find information regarding further requirements (e.g. qualitative admission requirements, letter of motivation, language proficiency, ...) on the overview page of the individual degree programme. To find the overview choose the programme you are interested in from the list at [Admission > Master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/).

You have to submit your application for admission to the degree programme within the application period. The application period depends on your citizenships. [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069) have a special application period.

selected.title: Master Access Guide (MAG)
selected.text:
Do you want to study a master programme at the University of Vienna? The prerequisite for this is the completion of an eligible bachelor or diploma programme (= in the same discipline) at a post-secondary educational institution.

In the MAG you’ll learn:

- which degree programmes are considered as eligible for your chosen degree programme
- if you need to fulfil any qualitative admission prerequisites for admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.
- if you need to fulfil any supplementary  examinations after admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.

If your previous degree is not listed in the Master Access Guide of the respective degree programme, your application will be checked individually as a decision has to be made on a case-by-case basis. To do this, you must apply for admission to the degree programme by the relevant deadline. The University of Vienna can only assess the eligibility of your previous degree on the basis of your complete application. Preliminary assessments are not possible.

The admission requirements according to university law and the applicable master [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) are binding.

[## You've got further questions?](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

[You get further information at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme.](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

selected.title: Information for asylum seekers
heading_path: How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
selected.text: How do I get admitted to a degree programme? In order to be admitted to a bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna you are required to possess a secondary-school leaving certificate or to have completed a degree programme of at least three years. Admission to master programmes require the completion of a subject-related bachelor programme. - You need to provide the required documents in their original or legalised form. If you no longer possess your secondary-school leaving certificate or your higher education degree certificate, you may request a substitute confirmation at the [Federal Ministry Women, Science and Research Republic of Austria](https://www.bmfwf.gv.at/public.html). Admission is not possible without a secondary-school leaving certificate or higher education degree certificate. - Documents issued in a language other than German or English additionally require a legally attested translation. Your documents do not require [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/), irrespective of the issuing country. However, you need to prove your status as a recognised refugee/asylum seeker (e.g. by means of a convention travel document). You can submit your application for admission online on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/). This is only possible during the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The University of Vienna conducts an [entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) for select bachelor/diploma programmes. An entrance exam procedure is also conducted for admission to the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. You can apply for admission to these programmes only once per academic year, in spring. The entrance

Choose a degree programme to receive detailed information regarding the admission procedure. Your steps vary depending on

- if you have completed your bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna or another university, and
- if there are any qualitative admission requirements (QZB) prescribed in the curriculum, and
- if a selection procedure (AV for "Aufnahmeverfahren") is conducted for the master programme of your choice, and

Use the selection below. There you will find a step-by-step introduction on how to submit your application and complete the admission procedure.

heading_path: D
selected.text: programmes, you will need subject-specific prior knowledge. You must provide proof of this when you apply for admission. Proof is provided either in the form of a trancript of records from the University of Vienna or a description of your qualifications (= a description of your achievements). The curricula of the master programmes or the Master Access Guide (MAG) indicate which extension curricula (EC) will provide you with the subject-specific prior knowledge. You have the following options: 1. If you have been admitted to a Bachelor's programme at the University of Vienna, you will already complete the required EC during the course of this programme. You do not need a qualification description, your transcript of records is sufficient proof. 2. You did not study at the University of Vienna? Compare the curriculum of your degree programme with the admission requirements listed in the EC curriculum and enter the relevant achievements from your previous degree programme in the "Description of Qualifications" form. Upload the form as proof with your application for admission. Related links: - [Master Access Guide (MAG)](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/#c167271) - [Admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876049) - [List of all curricula](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) (German only) - Diploma programme In 1999, the Bologna Process was initiated by the European Higher Education Area (EHEA). selected.title: Additional master programme
heading_path: Master programmes without entrance exam procedure
Note for graduates of the University of Vienna
selected.text:
The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

assemble.context:
[1] I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind? — I want to study a master programme > General requirements for the application for admission to master programmes
- scan of your valid passport or personal ID (front and back) as JPEG or PNG file
- - higher education degree certificate and transcripts/diploma supplement
    - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
    - and a sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)

  - for German and multilingual programmes: [proof of German language proficiency](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) at level A2 or higher
- if applicable proof of [English language proficiency/knowledge of other languages](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/) according to the curriculum
- further documents specified in the [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/#c124164) (e.g. description of qualifications)

You can find information regarding further requirements (e.g. qualitative admission requirements, letter of motivation, language proficiency, ...) on the overview page of the individual degree programme. To find the overview choose the programme you are interested in from the list at [Admission > Master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/).

You have to submit your application for admission to the degree programme within the application period. The application period depends on your citizenships. [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069) have a special application period.

[2] Master Access Guide (MAG)
Do you want to study a master programme at the University of Vienna? The prerequisite for this is the completion of an eligible bachelor or diploma programme (= in the same discipline) at a post-secondary educational institution.

In the MAG you’ll learn:

- which degree programmes are considered as eligible for your chosen degree programme
- if you need to fulfil any qualitative admission prerequisites for admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.
- if you need to fulfil any supplementary  examinations after admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.

If your previous degree is not listed in the Master Access Guide of the respective degree programme, your application will be checked individually as a decision has to be made on a case-by-case basis. To do this, you must apply for admission to the degree programme by the relevant deadline. The University of Vienna can only assess the eligibility of your previous degree on the basis of your complete application. Preliminary assessments are not possible.

The admission requirements according to university law and the applicable master [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) are binding.

[## You've got further questions?](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

[You get further information at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme.](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

[3] Information for asylum seekers — How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
How do I get admitted to a degree programme? In order to be admitted to a bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna you are required to possess a secondary-school leaving certificate or to have completed a degree programme of at least three years. Admission to master programmes require the completion of a subject-related bachelor programme. - You need to provide the required documents in their original or legalised form. If you no longer possess your secondary-school leaving certificate or your higher education degree certificate, you may request a substitute confirmation at the [Federal Ministry Women, Science and Research Republic of Austria](https://www.bmfwf.gv.at/public.html). Admission is not possible without a secondary-school leaving certificate or higher education degree certificate. - Documents issued in a language other than German or English additionally require a legally attested translation. Your documents do not require [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/), irrespective of the issuing country. However, you need to prove your status as a recognised refugee/asylum seeker (e.g. by means of a convention travel document). You can submit your application for admission online on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/). This is only possible during the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The University of Vienna conducts an [entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) for select bachelor/diploma programmes. An entrance exam procedure is also conducted for admission to the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. You can apply for admission to these programmes only once per academic year, in spring. The entrance

[4] Admission to master programmes
Choose a degree programme to receive detailed information regarding the admission procedure. Your steps vary depending on

- if you have completed your bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna or another university, and
- if there are any qualitative admission requirements (QZB) prescribed in the curriculum, and
- if a selection procedure (AV for "Aufnahmeverfahren") is conducted for the master programme of your choice, and
- if the language of instruction of the degree programme is English or German.

Use the selection below. There you will find a step-by-step introduction on how to submit your application and complete the admission procedure.

[5] ABC of terminology — D
programmes, you will need subject-specific prior knowledge. You must provide proof of this when you apply for admission. Proof is provided either in the form of a trancript of records from the University of Vienna or a description of your qualifications (= a description of your achievements). The curricula of the master programmes or the Master Access Guide (MAG) indicate which extension curricula (EC) will provide you with the subject-specific prior knowledge. You have the following options: 1. If you have been admitted to a Bachelor's programme at the University of Vienna, you will already complete the required EC during the course of this programme. You do not need a qualification description, your transcript of records is sufficient proof. 2. You did not study at the University of Vienna? Compare the curriculum of your degree programme with the admission requirements listed in the EC curriculum and enter the relevant achievements from your previous degree programme in the "Description of Qualifications" form. Upload the form as proof with your application for admission. Related links: - [Master Access Guide (MAG)](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/#c167271) - [Admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876049) - [List of all curricula](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) (German only) - Diploma programme In 1999, the Bologna Process was initiated by the European Higher Education Area (EHEA). This fundamentally changed the university structure in Austria. Diploma programmes have been replaced by bachelor and master programmes. At the University of Vienna,

[6] Additional master programme — Master programmes without entrance exam procedure > Note for graduates of the University of Vienna
The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Question: What are the basic requirements to be admitted to a master's programme at the University of Vienna?

generate.raw_response: To be admitted to a master's programme at the University of Vienna, the basic requirements include a higher education degree certificate and transcripts/diploma supplement [1], completion of an eligible bachelor or diploma programme in the same discipline [2], and proof of language proficiency (German at level A2 or higher for German and multilingual programmes, and English language proficiency if applicable) [1]. Additionally, further documents may be required as specified in the curriculum [1].

answer: To be admitted to a master's programme at the University of Vienna, the basic requirements include a higher education degree certificate and transcripts/diploma supplement [1], completion of an eligible bachelor or diploma programme in the same discipline [2], and proof of language proficiency (German at level A2 or higher for German and multilingual programmes, and English language proficiency if applicable) [1]. Additionally, further documents may be required as specified in the curriculum [1].
